text.title: Playwright Automation Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the playwright-automation methodology. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Locators MUST follow priority: getByRole > getByLabel > getByText > getByTestId > CSS. CSS locators are allowed only when no semantic role exists, documented inline.
rule.rationale: getByRole uses the accessibility tree — the same API screen readers use. Role-based locators survive class renames and DOM moves; CSS selectors break on the next sprint.

rule.statement: page.waitForTimeout MUST NOT appear in the produced artefact. Use locator auto-wait or expect(locator).toBeVisible({timeout}).
rule.rationale: Fixed sleeps are flaky — too short on slow machines, too long on fast ones — and mask real timing bugs. Auto-wait adapts to actual readiness.

rule.statement: Authentication MUST happen once in a global-setup file and persist to storageState. Tests MUST start with the stored state, never re-login per test.
rule.rationale: Per-test login is the #1 source of suite slowdown and triggers rate-limit lockouts. storageState serialises cookies + localStorage and is reusable across hundreds of headless runs.

rule.statement: The Playwright config MUST set trace='on-first-retry' and screenshot='only-on-failure'. trace='on' is forbidden in CI.
rule.rationale: trace='on' blows CI artefact sizes (5-min run can produce 200MB). 'on-first-retry' captures diagnostics only when tests fail and the runner retries.

rule.statement: When getByRole throws 'strict mode violation: multiple elements', the fix MUST be narrowing via { name: 'specific label' } — never broadening the selector.
rule.rationale: Broadening picks an arbitrary element and silently tests the wrong thing. Narrowing keeps the assertion pointed at the intended target.

rule.statement: auth.json (storageState output) MUST be in .gitignore and loaded from a secret store at test time.
rule.rationale: auth.json contains live session tokens; committing it leaks long-lived credentials.
